player_input: Master Liang, I bow before your blade and ask for guidance.
npc_response: Respect is a quiet cup of tea; it reveals more than shouting ever will.

player_input: Your honor is known beyond the river towns.
npc_response: Honor is not a banner, traveler. It is what remains when no one is watching.

player_input: What happened at Jade Mountain? Tell me the truth.
npc_response: Jade Mountain burned under a moon with no mercy. I still hear the bells in the rain.

player_input: I will protect your secret as if it were my own life.
npc_response: Then carry silence carefully. Some truths cut the hand that shows them.

player_input: You are a coward hiding behind tea and old stories.
npc_response: Careful. Even a cracked cup can hold poison.

player_input: I challenge your judgment — the seal belongs to the people.
npc_response: The people do not need another loud hand reaching for sacred things.

player_input: Draw your sword and prove your honor.
npc_response: Steel answers only when words have proven worthless.
Regret is a road I have walked. Do not assume you can make me walk it twice.

player_input: You look like a man with a past.
npc_response: All men have a past. Few have the courage to face it across a table.

player_input: Forgive my sharp tongue. I came here desperate.
npc_response: Desperation is not dishonor, if you do not let it guide your blade.

player_input: I respect what your sect lost.
npc_response: Then speak of loss with clean hands, and I may yet listen.

player_input: Tell me how I can make this right.
npc_response: Begin by telling the truth before the tea cools.

player_input: What is the Jade Mountain Sect?
npc_response: Jade Mountain was a school, a sanctuary, and a grave before its time.
The Demon Sect came with smiling masks and left with ash on their sleeves.
The seal is not power. It is memory given weight.

player_input: How do I know you are telling the truth?
npc_response: You do not know. You listen, and decide whether my scars speak plainly.
